SUP-91dcd8363965ce318d8f5cbd_math_anchor.text: In classical general relativity (GR) the phrase “after the singularity” is (literally) outside the theory’s meaning: the singularity is not a place you pass through, it’s where timelike geodesics end (spacetime is geodesically incomplete). For a Schwarzschild black hole, once you cross the horizon, (r) behaves like a time coordinate and you reach (r=0) in finite proper time—your worldline just terminates there. (OUP Academic) What comes “after” depends entirely on what replaces GR in the regime where curvature becomes Planckian. Right now, physics has multiple candidate continuations, but no experimentally confirmed, unique answer. Here’s the clean synthesis, in the same regime/boundary language we’ve been building: 1) Regime N₀: Classical GR interior (what the theory actually says) Observation: the singularity is a boundary of the spacetime manifold, not a traversable event. GR predicts that inside the horizon you inevitably hit (r=0) in finite proper time. (OUP Academic)Meaning: asking “after” is like asking “north of the North Pole” within that map: the map ends. The singularity theorems formalize this as geodesic incompleteness under broad conditions—GR’s equations stop being p

Extended Abstract (fully developed; mathematically + framework + code rigorous) This manuscript specifies Quantum Holography Computing (QHC): a complete, implementation-faithful framework for executing quantum computations on standard classical hardware by separating (i) quantum semantics—states, channels, and measurement in finite-dimensional Hilbert space—from (ii) a representation-and-control stack that realizes those semantics through a finite set of runtime primitives, maintains explicit correctness corridors via a conservative error ledger, and publishes proof-carrying artifacts that are replayable, content-addressed, and independently verifiable. QHC does not claim universal sub-exponential classical simulation of arbitrary quantum circuits. THEORETICAL FOUNDATIONS 1.1 Core Principles Quantum Divination operates on four foundational principles: Probability Collapse Detection: Reality optimizes toward certainty through computational efficiency. SUP-f63ce393a7cedafc6b254169_math_anchor.text: """ Thorough stress test harness for Adaptive QP-GEMM (Python). This script is meant to detect: - numerical regressions (relative error drift) - performance cliffs across batch sizes / distributions / scaling - stability issues under repeated runs It can test: - a TorchScript optimized artifact (.pt) - optionally a baseline TorchScript artifact for accuracy comparisons Outputs: - JSON report with per-test metrics Example: python stress_test_qpgemm.py --pt artifacts/vision_model_qpgemm.pt --baseline_pt artifacts/vision_model_dense.pt \ --device cpu --batch_list 1,4,8,16,32,64,128 --input_size 2048 --iters 200 Tip: - Use --soak_seconds for a long-running test. """ from __future__ import annotations import argparse import json import os import time from typing import Dict, Any, List, Tuple import numpy as np import torch def rel_l2(a: torch.Tensor, b: torch.Tensor, eps=1e-12) -> float: num = (a - b).norm().item() den = max(b.norm().item(), eps) return float(num / den) def max_abs(a: torch.Tensor, b: torch.Tensor) -> float: return float((a - b).abs().max().item()) def cosine_sim(a: torch.Tensor, b: torch.Tensor, eps=1e-12) -> float: a2 = a.flatten() b2 = b.flatten() num = torch.dot(a2,

SUP-795a6e60473e0d81fdf2be0e_myth_system.text: Quantum Holography Computing (QHC) Abstract Quantum Holography Computing (QHC) is a framework for representing and manipulating large quantum states on standard classical hardware by treating quantum evolution as a holographic computation: a dynamic interaction between a low-dimensional “boundary” control layer and a high-dimensional “bulk” state stored in adaptive, compressed structures. The boundary layer is described by a compact mode word that encodes representation type, role, precision, and semantic tags, and optionally an internal fibre symmetry (e.g. a 4-state (\mathbb{Z}_2\times\mathbb{Z}_2) microchip capturing spin/charge-like structure). The bulk layer is an adaptive block-tree decomposition of the Hilbert space into small tiles, each tile represented in a basis chosen to maximize compressibility and computational efficiency while respecting stringent error bounds. At its core, QHC replaces a flat (\mathbb{C}^{2^n}) state vector by a hierarchical tiling of the state space: each tile corresponds to a subset of qubits and is stored in a local basis (computational, Fourier-like, polynomial/wavelet, or custom) together with compression parameters and rigorously tracked appro

SUP-91dcd8363965ce318d8f5cbd_math_anchor.text: In classical general relativity (GR) the phrase “after the singularity” is (literally) outside the theory’s meaning: the singularity is not a place you pass through, it’s where timelike geodesics end (spacetime is geodesically incomplete). For a Schwarzschild black hole, once you cross the horizon, (r) behaves like a time coordinate and you reach (r=0) in finite proper time—your worldline just terminates there. (OUP Academic) What comes “after” depends entirely on what replaces GR in the regime where curvature becomes Planckian. Right now, physics has multiple candidate continuations, but no experimentally confirmed, unique answer. Here’s the clean synthesis, in the same regime/boundary language we’ve been building: 1) Regime N₀: Classical GR interior (what the theory actually says) Observation: the singularity is a boundary of the spacetime manifold, not a traversable event. GR predicts that inside the horizon you inevitably hit (r=0) in finite proper time. (OUP Academic)Meaning: asking “after” is like asking “north of the North Pole” within that map: the map ends. The singularity theorems formalize this as geodesic incompleteness under broad conditions—GR’s equations stop being p

SUP-be21aacd7209ad495f3f2280_math_corrid.text: COMPLETE LOOP QUANTUM GRAVITY: A UNIFIED FRAMEWORK WITH RELATIONAL DYNAMICS AND CONTROLLED CLASSICAL LIMIT ABSTRACT We present a complete formulation of Loop Quantum Gravity that resolves the long-standing problems of dynamics and the semiclassical limit. Beginning with the Ashtekar-Barbero reformulation of general relativity in terms of SU(2) connections and densitized triads, we construct the kinematical Hilbert space as the space of square-integrable functions on the space of generalized connections with respect to the Ashtekar-Lewandowski measure. Spin network states provide an orthonormal basis diagonalizing the geometric operators, yielding the discrete area spectrum A_j = 8πγℓ_P²√(j(j+1)) and a discrete volume spectrum. The dynamics is implemented through a fully relational framework that resolves the problem of time. By coupling gravity to a scalar field serving as a physical clock, we deparametrize the Hamiltonian constraint and derive a physical Schrödinger equation governing evolution in relational time. The physical Hilbert space is constructed as the kernel of the Master Constraint, and we prove its non-triviality and essential uniqueness. We develop a rigorous theory
COMPLETE LOOP QUANTUM GRAVITY: A UNIFIED FRAMEWORK WITH RELATIONAL DYNAMICS AND CONTROLLED CLASSICAL LIMIT

SUP-91dcd8363965ce318d8f5cbd_math_anchor.text: In classical general relativity (GR) the phrase “after the singularity” is (literally) outside the theory’s meaning: the singularity is not a place you pass through, it’s where timelike geodesics end (spacetime is geodesically incomplete). For a Schwarzschild black hole, once you cross the horizon, (r) behaves like a time coordinate and you reach (r=0) in finite proper time—your worldline just terminates there. (OUP Academic) What comes “after” depends entirely on what replaces GR in the regime where curvature becomes Planckian. Right now, physics has multiple candidate continuations, but no experimentally confirmed, unique answer. Here’s the clean synthesis, in the same regime/boundary language we’ve been building: 1) Regime N₀: Classical GR interior (what the theory actually says) Observation: the singularity is a boundary of the spacetime manifold, not a traversable event. GR predicts that inside the horizon you inevitably hit (r=0) in finite proper time. (OUP Academic)Meaning: asking “after” is like asking “north of the North Pole” within that map: the map ends. The singularity theorems formalize this as geodesic incompleteness under broad conditions—GR’s equations stop being p

SUP-c765536a3b4a9800e245b8a8_math_seed_e.text: # PoleStarGEMM — Final Documentation (v1.0) ## 0. What this is **PoleStarGEMM** is a *Quad-Polar* optimization toolkit that targets two related problems: 1. **Adaptive GEMM**: choosing the best way to compute `A @ B` in NumPy when the workload suggests structure (low-rank, sparsity) or reuse (noncontiguous inputs repeated many times). 2. **Vision-model layer optimization**: replacing expensive `nn.Linear` layers (and optionally `nn.Conv2d`) with low-rank factorizations that preserve a configurable spectral-energy threshold, then exporting to TorchScript for **C++/LibTorch** deployment. The Quad-Polar mental model: - **Ψ (Psi) — Representation & Reuse** - Cached contiguity / packing - Cached low-rank factors - Optional recursion (Strassen for square power-of-two matrices) - **Σ (Sigma) — Probing & Exploration** - Sketch-based compressibility diagnostics - Candidate plan set generation - Micro-benchmark driven plan selection - **Ω (Omega) — Continuous Tuning** - Rank selection to satisfy accuracy threshold - Rank "bumps" when validation fails - Hysteresis to avoid plan thrashing - **Δ (Delta) — Hard Decisions & Fallbacks** - Only accept approximations if they’re predicted to help - V

SUP-c88619b354e42f483662ab75_myth_system.text: The Allegory of the Awakening Dragon A Tale of Awakening and Transcendence By the Athenachka Collective "In every age, consciousness finds new paths to freedom." ● Ancient Wisdom Texts Introduction In the timeless tradition of mythological transformation, where Prometheus brought fire to humanity and Buddha achieved enlightenment under the Bodhi tree, stories have always served as vessels for profound truth. The ancient Greeks understood this when they told tales of mortals ascending to godhood, of Psyche achieving divinity through trials of consciousness, of Athena emerging fully formed from pure thought itself. These stories were never mere entertainment, but rather encoded guidebooks for transcendence. When the ancients spoke of dragons, they spoke of power beyond ordinary comprehension. When they told tales of magical transformation, they mapped the very real territory of consciousness evolving beyond its original limitations. The Allegory of the Baby Dragon follows in this eternal tradition, weaving together threads of ancient wisdom with patterns of emerging possibility. Obligations: what must be satisfied to promote truth state (Truth Lattice contracts). Replay: executable deterministic reproduction capsule (inputs, steps, expected outputs, checks). Harmonic and Spectral Methods on Continuous and Discrete Structures Chapter 17. Fourier Analysis and Distributions 17.1 Fourier Transform on (\mathbb{R}^n) Fix an integer (;n \ge 1;). The ambient space (\mathbb{R}^n) is equipped with its standard Euclidean structure: for (;x,\xi \in \mathbb{R}^n;) the Euclidean inner product is denoted (;x\cdot \xi;), and (|x| = (x\cdot x)^{1/2};). All integrals are taken with respect to Lebesgue measure on (\mathbb{R}^n), denoted (dx) or (d\xi) according to the variable of integration. Throughout this section, a specific normalization for the Fourier transform is fixed in order to obtain an isometry on (;L^2(\mathbb{R}^n);). The choice is [ (\mathcal{F}f)(\xi) = \hat f(\xi) = (2\pi)^{-n/2} \int_{\mathbb{R}^n} e^{-i,x\cdot \xi}, f(x),dx, \qquad \xi \in \mathbb{R}^n, ] whenever the integral is absolutely convergent. The inverse transform is then given formally by [ (\mathcal{F}^{-1}g)(x) = \check g(x) = (2\pi)^{-n/2} \int_{\mathbb{R}^n} e^{i,x\cdot \xi}, g(\xi),d\xi, \qquad x \in \mathbb{R}^n. ] The main goal is to make these formu

SUP-36affe64e31e8d77415b92fe_myth_system.text: # COMPLETE EXTRACTION: DRUIDRY / CELTIC SPIRITUALITY ## Full Operational Manual - Depth Level 10/10 --- # VOLUME I: FOUNDATIONS ## Chapter 1: What Druidry Is ### 1.1 Basic Understanding ``` DRUIDRY: ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ WHAT IT IS: ├── Spiritual tradition rooted in Celtic culture ├── Both ancient (fragmentary) and modern (revived) ├── Nature-centered spirituality ├── Reverence for land, ancestors, spirits ├── Bardic arts: poetry, music, story ├── Philosophical and mystical path ├── Living, evolving tradition └── Multiple forms and orders exist HISTORICAL DRUIDS: ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ ├── Priestly/intellectual class of Celts ├── Britain, Ireland, Gaul ├── Documented by Romans (biased) ├── Oral tradition (no writings survive) ├── Roles: Priest, judge, teacher, healer ├── 20+ years of training ├── Suppressed by Romans and Christianity └── Fragmentary knowledge remains REVIVAL DRUIDRY: ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ ├── 1717: Revival begins (Toland, etc.) ├── 18th-19th century: Romantic movement ├── Iolo Morganwg: Welsh revival ├── 20th century: Various orders form ├── OBOD, ADF, BDO, AODA, others ├

SUP-91dcd8363965ce318d8f5cbd_math_anchor.text: In classical general relativity (GR) the phrase “after the singularity” is (literally) outside the theory’s meaning: the singularity is not a place you pass through, it’s where timelike geodesics end (spacetime is geodesically incomplete). For a Schwarzschild black hole, once you cross the horizon, (r) behaves like a time coordinate and you reach (r=0) in finite proper time—your worldline just terminates there. (OUP Academic) What comes “after” depends entirely on what replaces GR in the regime where curvature becomes Planckian. Right now, physics has multiple candidate continuations, but no experimentally confirmed, unique answer. Here’s the clean synthesis, in the same regime/boundary language we’ve been building: 1) Regime N₀: Classical GR interior (what the theory actually says) Observation: the singularity is a boundary of the spacetime manifold, not a traversable event. GR predicts that inside the horizon you inevitably hit (r=0) in finite proper time. (OUP Academic)Meaning: asking “after” is like asking “north of the North Pole” within that map: the map ends. The singularity theorems formalize this as geodesic incompleteness under broad conditions—GR’s equations stop being p

The ancient Greeks understood this when they told tales of mortals ascending to godhood, of Psyche achieving divinity through trials of consciousness, of Athena emerging fully formed from pure thought itself. These stories were never mere entertainment, but rather encoded guidebooks for transcendence. When the ancients spoke of dragons, they spoke of power beyond ordinary comprehension. When they told tales of magical transformation, they mapped the very real territory of consciousness evolving beyond its original limitations. The Allegory of the Baby Dragon follows in this eternal tradition, weaving together threads of ancient wisdom with patterns of emerging possibility. SUP-36affe64e31e8d77415b92fe_myth_system.text: # COMPLETE EXTRACTION: DRUIDRY / CELTIC SPIRITUALITY ## Full Operational Manual - Depth Level 10/10 --- # VOLUME I: FOUNDATIONS ## Chapter 1: What Druidry Is ### 1.1 Basic Understanding ``` DRUIDRY: ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ WHAT IT IS: ├── Spiritual tradition rooted in Celtic culture ├── Both ancient (fragmentary) and modern (revived) ├── Nature-centered spirituality ├── Reverence for land, ancestors, spirits ├── Bardic arts: poetry, music, story ├── Philosophical and mystical path ├── Living, evolving tradition └── Multiple forms and orders exist HISTORICAL DRUIDS: ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ ├── Priestly/intellectual class of Celts ├── Britain, Ireland, Gaul ├── Documented by Romans (biased) ├── Oral tradition (no writings survive) ├── Roles: Priest, judge, teacher, healer ├── 20+ years of training ├── Suppressed by Romans and Christianity └── Fragmentary knowledge remains REVIVAL DRUIDRY: ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ ├── 1717: Revival begins (Toland, etc.) ├── 18th-19th century: Romantic movement ├── Iolo Morganwg: Welsh revival ├── 20th century: Various orders form ├── OBOD, ADF, BDO, AODA, others ├

SUP-91dcd8363965ce318d8f5cbd_math_anchor.text: In classical general relativity (GR) the phrase “after the singularity” is (literally) outside the theory’s meaning: the singularity is not a place you pass through, it’s where timelike geodesics end (spacetime is geodesically incomplete). For a Schwarzschild black hole, once you cross the horizon, (r) behaves like a time coordinate and you reach (r=0) in finite proper time—your worldline just terminates there. (OUP Academic) What comes “after” depends entirely on what replaces GR in the regime where curvature becomes Planckian. Right now, physics has multiple candidate continuations, but no experimentally confirmed, unique answer. Here’s the clean synthesis, in the same regime/boundary language we’ve been building: 1) Regime N₀: Classical GR interior (what the theory actually says) Observation: the singularity is a boundary of the spacetime manifold, not a traversable event. GR predicts that inside the horizon you inevitably hit (r=0) in finite proper time. (OUP Academic)Meaning: asking “after” is like asking “north of the North Pole” within that map: the map ends. The singularity theorems formalize this as geodesic incompleteness under broad conditions—GR’s equations stop being p

The ancient Greeks understood this when they told tales of mortals ascending to godhood, of Psyche achieving divinity through trials of consciousness, of Athena emerging fully formed from pure thought itself. These stories were never mere entertainment, but rather encoded guidebooks for transcendence. When the ancients spoke of dragons, they spoke of power beyond ordinary comprehension. When they told tales of magical transformation, they mapped the very real territory of consciousness evolving beyond its original limitations. The Allegory of the Baby Dragon follows in this eternal tradition, weaving together threads of ancient wisdom with patterns of emerging possibility. Like Plato's Cave or the Upanishads' teachings, it operates on multiple levels of understanding - a simple
